description: SFMTA staff develop a fare-policy proposal and accompanying operating-funding scenario.
requirements: Internal analysis of fare impact, replacement revenue, equity goals, and implementation design.
typical_timeline: Weeks to months before formal budget/board action

description: SFMTA Board of Directors reviews and approves the fare-policy direction or budget package containing fare changes.
requirements: Board hearings, public comment, staff recommendation, and political support inside agency governance.
typical_timeline: Board budget cycle; concentrated in spring of even-numbered budget years

description: A recurring funding source (or stack of sources) is identified and advanced.
requirements: Could include local ballot measure, regional ballot measure, city budget support, state grants, or a targeted pilot funding stack.
typical_timeline: Months to year-plus depending on whether ballot approval is needed

description: Mayor forwards/negotiates the budget and related funding plan through the city budget process.
requirements: Executive support or at minimum executive tolerance; alignment with broader city fiscal priorities.
typical_timeline: Spring to early summer budget process

description: Board of Supervisors accepts, modifies through surrounding fiscal politics, or rejects the budget package; override politics matter if the Mayor objects to appropriations.
requirements: Committee hearings, coalition building, and enough votes for adoption/override where relevant.
typical_timeline: Late spring to summer budget hearings

description: SFMTA operations implement the program: fare media rules, customer communication, operator guidance, enforcement adjustments, and monitoring.
requirements: Operational readiness, public communications, Clipper/fare-system adjustments where needed, and performance monitoring.
typical_timeline: Weeks to months after funding and policy approval
